select_when: An experienced team keeps reaching for the same proven frame even though the situation feels structurally different.
The first solution arrived suspiciously fast and there is reason to think expertise is blinding the group to alternatives.
A problem requires reframing, cross-domain analogies, or deliberate interruption of the dominant mental template.
People are assuming they have seen this one before without pressure-testing that assumption.
avoid_when: Avoid-fixation language is becoming a reason to reject all prior knowledge instead of distinguishing helpful pattern recognition from stale lock-in.
The team already has strong evidence that the familiar pattern truly fits and is using anti-fixation rhetoric to keep reopening a settled question.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on stale pattern lock-in, expertise traps, and deliberate de-fixation rather than on generic creativity slogans.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader learning-science sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on fixation and frame interruption.
open_questions: The source overlaps with teaching and creativity, but the operational file stays on stale-solution lock-in.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on experienced teams reusing old frames, suspiciously fast answers, and the need for reframing or interruption.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against overcorrecting by discarding all prior knowledge instead of distinguishing fresh pattern recognition from stale fixation.

input_type.note: The typed label compresses the raw fixation conditions into a stable routing identity.

output_type.note: The output label captures the intervention as de-fixation rather than generic ideation.

reasoning_types.note: The model relies on reflective interruption of a stuck frame and counterfactual alternative search.
